data.title: India Central Bank Tipped to Begin Easing Cycle, WSJ Poll Shows — Market Talk
data.description: India's Central Bank Is Tipped to Cut Its Policy Repo Rate by 25 Bps to 6.25% on Friday, According to All 12 Economists Polled by The Wall Street Journal. The government's focus on narrowing the fiscal deficit, and high frequency indicators pointing to a growth slowdown are key factors supporting expectations of a rate cut, DBS Group Research's Radhika Rao says in a note. With inflation falling, room for rate cuts and easier liquidity has opened up, HSBC economists say. Citi Research analysts expect a 25bp cut, highlighting that this has been the longest period the RBI has been on hold after the end of a rate hike cycle. However, Citi notes some investors still expect the RBI to stay on hold due to global uncertainties. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

data.title: Mitsubishi Chemical 3Q Basic Materials, Polymers Core Op Loss Y0.8B Vs Profit Y0.8B
A.P. Møller - Mærsk to Initiate DKK14 Billion Share Buyback Program
data.description:
A.P. Møller - Mærsk (MAERSK-A.CO, MAERSK-B.CO) said Wednesday it plans to commence a 12-month share buyback program totaling 14.4 billion Danish kroner.

The first phase will commence on Friday and run until Aug. 6 with 7.2 billion kroner worth of shares to be repurchased. Skandinaviska Enskilda Banken was appointed as lead manager for the initial phase.

The shipping giant said the program is intended to adjust its capital structure with the repurchased shares to be ultimately canceled.

data.title: Tecom Group Logs Higher FY24 Profit, Revenue
data.description:
Tecom Group (DFM:TECOM) said Thursday that its profit and revenue for the full-year 2024 climbed year over year.

Profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 1.23 billion Emirati dirhams, compared with 1.08 billion dirhams earlier. EPS moved to 0.25 dirham from 0.22 dirham.

The Dubai-listed property developer's revenue was 2.40 billion dirhams, compared with 2.17 billion dirhams previously.

Meanwhile, the board proposed an unchanged cash dividend of 0.08 dirham per share for the period, subject to approval from shareholders at an upcoming annual general meeting on March 10.

The board also reviewed the interim cash dividend for the second half of 2025, which is expected to increase by 10%.

data.title: Guosen Securities' Asset Management Arm Secures Securities, Futures Business License
data.description:
Guosen Securities' subsidiary, Guosen Securities Asset Management, obtained a license to operate securities and futures businesses, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Thursday.

The scope of the securities and futures business is securities asset management. The asset management unit will also be allowed to operate as a securities brokerage, securities investment consultant and financial consultant for securities trading and other similar functions, the Chinese brokerage said.

Guosen Securities' shares rose more than 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Market Chatter: Singapore to Contribute $87 Million to IDA
data.description:
Singapore has pledged to inject $87 million in International Development Association across three years, according to a Singapore Business Review report on Wednesday.

According to second minister of finance, Indranee Rajah, the amount represents a 24% increase from the country's previous contribution.

IDA is a World Bank project that focuses on providing grants and low-interest loans to low-income countries, the report said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: India's benchmarks pause ahead of key earnings, RBI rate decision
data.description:
India's benchmark indexes were little changed on Thursday, as investors awaited key corporate earnings and the Reserve Bank of India's rate decision.

The Nifty 50 was off 0.14% at 23,664.9 points as of 9:47 a.m. IST, while the BSE Sensex had slipped 0.14% to 78,158.1.

They had opened about 0.3% higher after logging marginal losses in the previous session, with sentiment remaining neutral ahead of the RBI's widely expected first interest rate cut in nearly five years.

A rate cut, if it happens, will follow less than a week after the government, in the federal budget, cut personal tax rates to stimulate consumption.

The benchmarks have taken "a pause after the rebound" on Tuesday and are awaiting the RBI's rate decision and commentary and some key Nifty 50 companies for directional triggers, said Prashanth Tapse, senior vice president of research at Mehta Equities.

Six Nifty companies — State Bank of India , Trent , Bharti Airtel , Hero MotoCorp , Britannia and ITC — are due to report their quarterly earnings on the day.

Eight of the 13 major sectors declined in morning trade.

The broader, more domestically focused small-caps and mid-caps traded flat, erasing opening gains.

Among individual stocks, food delivery and quick commerce platform Swiggy lost about 8% to a record low after reporting a wider loss in the third quarter.

Winemaker Sula Vineyards dropped 4% after posting a 35% slide in its December-quarter profit, hurt by a slowdown in urban consumption.

Trucking firm VRL Logistics jumped 14% after logging a rise in quarterly profit.

Hawkish BOJ Board Member Calls for Potentially Faster Rate Hikes — Update
data.description:
By Megumi Fujikawa

A hawkish member of the Bank of Japan's policy board on Thursday called for potentially faster interest-rate increases, sending the yen to its strongest level against the dollar in eight weeks.

In a speech to business leaders in Nagano prefecture in central Japan, Naoki Tamura said that the central bank should raise rates to 1% or higher in the fiscal half starting in October. That level is likely consistent with a neutral rate setting that is neither restrictive nor stimulating for the economy, he added.

"Raising short-term interest rates to at least around 1% in the latter half of fiscal 2025 is necessary to reduce upside risk to prices and achieve the price stability target in a sustainable and stable manner," he said.

Tamura's comments suggest that the central bank may tighten monetary policy more quickly than some economists and investors have been expecting. Consensus views are for one or two more 25-basis-point hikes in the next fiscal year, but the board member suggested that the policy rate could go beyond 1%.

After his speech, the yen went to around 151.80 against the dollar, the strongest level since Dec. 11.

Many economists expect the BOJ to raise the policy rate again later this year, but some have cautioned that the next move requires careful consideration as rates approach levels Japan hasn't experienced in 30 years.

Tamura is a former executive at Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group and considered hawkish. He proposed a rate increase in December, citing increased risks of higher consumer prices. The proposal was defeated by a majority vote, but the policy board then decided to lift interest rates to 0.5% from 0.25% at the bank's January meeting.

Tamura's comments could well be testing the waters, seeing how markets react to the idea of rate hikes at least every six months, said Mizuho Securities economist Yusuke Matsuo.

The central bank has been making efforts to improve its communication to markets, with the goal of minimizing disruptive reactions to its policy moves.

That said, Matsuo thinks the BOJ may have to stop once rates reach 0.75% because he expects inflation to slow.

Tamura seems to have a different outlook, saying in the speech that the bank should be able to achieve its goal of creating sustainable, stable 2% inflation in the second half of fiscal 2025 when wage trends among small companies become clearer.

Growth and mild inflation backed by higher wages is a central goal for the BOJ.

Tamura also voiced concern that inflation--which has been above 2% for nearly three years--and a recent spike in rice prices may hurt consumer sentiment. Consumer inflation picked up to 3.6% on year in December, compared with 2.9% in November.

Even if the BOJ raises interest rates to 0.75%, monetary conditions will remain easy as real interest rates will stay negative, the board member said.

Write to Megumi Fujikawa at megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com

data.title: Patronus Resources Identifies Base Metals at Western Australia Project, Shares Rise 6%
data.description:
Patronus Resources said exploration at its Cardinia project in Western Australia identified multiple anomalies indicative of volcanogenic massive Sulfide mineralization, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

It intersected zones of zinc and copper anomalism intersected, including intercepts of 8 meters at 2,013 parts-per-million grade of zinc and 366.15 ppm grade of copper from a depth of 79 meters as well as 4 meters at 2,643 ppm grade of zinc and 92.13 ppm grade of copper from 86 meters.

The exploration included induced polarization geophysical surveys, mapping and geochemical sampling, reverse circulation drilling and down-hole electromagnetic surveys.

It is planning to conduct follow-up drilling in the June quarter.

The firm's shares rose 6% in recent trading on Thursday.

Cettire Says JPMorgan Chase Becomes Substantial Shareholder; Shares Fall 5%
data.description:
Cettire received notice that JPMorgan Chase and its affiliated entities became substantial holders of the mining firm as of Feb. 3, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The financial services firm and its affiliates acquired 19.1 million shares in the company, representing 5% total voting power, the filing said.

Shares of the company fell nearly 5% in recent Thursday trade.

data.title: Arika Resources Identifies Potential of High-Grade Mineralization at Western Australia Gold Project, Shares Rise 7%
data.description:
Arika Resources said results from a surface rock chip sampling program at its Yundamindra gold project in Western Australia showed potential for high-grade mineralization, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

The program was designed as a first-pass assessment of new areas highlighted from structural targeting and it returned high-grade rock chip samples of up to 19.3 grams-per-tonne grade of gold.

The firm's shares rose past 7% in recent trading on Thursday.

data.title: U-Haul Holding Co reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• U-Haul Holding Co reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 30 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $5.06. The mean expectation of two analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 48 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 3.7% to $1.39 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $1.35 billion.

  • U-Haul Holding Co shares had risen by 2.9% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is no "strong buy" or "buy," 2 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The average consensus recommendation for the ground freight & logistics peer group is also "hold"

Wall Street's median 12-month price target for U-Haul Holding Co is $75.40

data.title: Singapore Reports 21% Increase in International Visitors During 2024
data.description:
Singapore booked a 21% incrase in international visitors during 2024, according to a release by the Singapore Tourism Board on Wednesday.

STB expects around 18.5 million visitors during 2025.

In the first nine months of 2024, tourist spending came in at SG$22.4 billion and the board expects spending to be between SG$27.5 billion and SG$29.0 billion in the third quarter of 2024.

A majority of arrivals were from China, Indonesia and India while China, Indonesia and Australia were the top sources of spending in the city-state.

data.title: Market Chatter: Lloyds to Challenge Court Ruling in GBP1 Billion Tax Case Linked to Winddown of Irish Operations
data.description:
Lloyds Banking Group intends to appeal a tribunal's decision that it owes the UK 1 billion pounds sterling in back taxes over the winddown of its operations in Ireland more than 10 years ago, Bloomberg News reported Wednesday, citing the bank.

The dispute stemmed from the British lender's exit of its Irish operations in 2010. The bank believes it is entitled to 3.8 billion pounds in tax relief but the tribunal disagreed.

People familiar with the matter told Bloomberg that the case would be transferred to the upper tax tribunal and that executives at the bank would not expect a resolution for years.

Amid the latest development in the years-long dispute, Lloyds is expected to recognize a cash reduction in its earnings, a person familiar with the matter said, noting that the tribunal's decision will not have any capital impact on the bank.

Lloyds did not immediately respond to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Mitsubishi Chemical Maintains Revenue, Net-Profit Views for FY Ending March
China's Retail Sales Growth May Slow Post Lunar New Year — Market Talk
data.description: China's retail sales growth may slow after the Lunar New Year holiday break, say Citi analysts in a research note. Sales of major retailer and catering companies during the Lunar New Year grew 4.1%, the analysts say, citing official data. Retail sales after the holiday period is likely to decelerate more dramatically, compared with the period before and during the Lunar New Year, where heavy promotions were implemented. The retail sales over the period January to mid-February would be more meaningful for investors to gauge the underlying consumption recovery than the short-term sales performance during the Lunar New Year break, they add. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: Fujian Zhangzhou Development Appoints Board Chairman
data.description:
Fujian Zhangzhou Development appointed Chen Yijian as the board's chairman, according to a Thursday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

Chen resigned earlier as the company's general manager. Both changes are effective Feb. 5.

data.title: India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at 10:00 a.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was down 0.2% to 78098, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 declined 0.2% to 23640, as investors awaited key corporate earnings and the Reserve Bank of India's rate decision on Friday for near-term directional triggers.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee hit its lifetime low of 87.5650 versus the U.S. dollar, pressured by a prevailing bearish bias and heightened dollar bids spurred by maturity of positions in the NDF market.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN067934G=CC) was quoted at 100.96 rupees, with the yield down 1 bp at 6.652%, a day ahead of a widely-anticipated interest rate cut.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was 1 basis points at 6.295%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) declined 2 bps to 6.04%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 6.45%, and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 6.26%

data.title: RMR Group Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• RMR Group Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 35 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 49 cents. The mean expectation of four analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 35 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 34 cents to 35 cents per share.

  • Revenue fell 16.1% to $219.48 million from a year ago; analysts expected $268.50 million.

  • RMR Group Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was 38 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $6.38 million.

  • RMR Group Inc shares had fallen by 8.0% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 5% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, two analysts negatively revised earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is no "strong buy" or "buy," 3 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The average consensus recommendation for the real estate services peer group is also "hold"

Wall Street's median 12-month price target for RMR Group Inc is $24.00

Zydus Lifesciences Posts Rise in Fiscal Q3 Consolidated Net Profit
data.description:
Zydus Lifesciences recorded a rise in its consolidated net profit to 10.2 billion Indian rupees in the fiscal third quarter ended Dec. 31, 2024, against 7.90 billion rupees a year ago.

Earnings per share came in at 10.17 rupees compared with the year-ago figure of 7.80 rupees, according to a Wednesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

Revenue from operations in fiscal Q3 increased to 52.7 billion rupees from 45.1 billion rupees a year earlier.

Shares of the company were up 2% in recent trade.
